reviews.review_title: Best VR Game I’ve played in a while
reviews.review_description:
I am a fan of Hello Neighbor, and this is one of the ONLY games that I could beat without a walkthrough. The puzzles are designed almost perfectly, and have real logic to them unlike the other games.  I really like this game because it doesn’t feel repetitive and goes on for a while, which is a very common problem I have with VR Games, which is that they are too short and feel repetitive.

I really like character switch mechanic and making the characters work together. The neighbor is there to be an obstacle but he isn’t so annoying that he ruins the game by making it nearly impossible. The lore sections are pretty scary too when the neighbor jumpscares you out of nowhere.

However there are some glitches that can be annoying, but they are pretty avoidable and you can just load the last auto save or switch characters to get unglitched. But I’m still rating it a 5-star because I don’t think glitches should be in the games rating.

reviews.review_title: Hello neighbor, search, and rescue is so
reviews.review_description: I love it. They made a lot of good job on Hello Neighbor search and rescue. It’s a very fun game and pretty scary. I love it 10 out of 10 I so love it. I love the neighbor the neighbors look so different. The character of the neighbor it’s so cool and we could become any kid but except for the neighbor it has more AI than though original neighbor his bear he has a beard and it’s so cool 10 out of 10 it’s so scary of the jump scare very very scary, but I love the details on it 👌👌👌👌👌👌👌👌🤩🤩🤩￼

Hello Neighbor has been one of my favorite video games as far back as I remember. This game serves the Hello Neighbor franchise well, and is a great window into the lore of the game. I have 100%ed Hello Neighbor, Hello Neighbor 2, and now Hello Neighbor Search and Rescue. This is one of my favorite VR games and I would highly recommend it to everyone.

I used to play this on psvr and encountered many bugs (flying of the map, softlocks ect) but I've converted to quest since and minus a bit of jank the game has improved, in my 3 playthroughs I haven't encountered anything game breaking. The neighbor is a lot scarier in vr especially since you are now a doll compared to his size he is also a lot faster than you so if you get spotted just pray. Compared to flat-screen games of hn this one is scarier, harder and there are consequences to being caught. For example all items you have before getting caught or any keys you leave on the ground the neighbor will pick up and hide them. 8 out of 10

reviews.review_title: Great but more likely better on PCVR
reviews.review_description: I have played through almost all the hello neighbor games excluding Hello engineer. This I can say confidently is one of the best hello neighbor games ever but not without imperfections, on launch this was pretty glitchy, they fixed most of the big glitches but besides that I can say if you have a computer capable of playing VR and this game play it with a link cable hooked to your computer, it gives a generally better experience but besides all that it’s still a great game both on quest and quest link, The graphics the Oculus Quest 2 (The headset I played on) are actually some of the better quality then the other games I have tried. They aren’t the best but are pretty good, and the graphics on PCVR at least on Steam were also very good and without surprise better then Quest but there aren’t very many options to adjust them to run well, and the anti aliasing setting completely breaks the entire game making all the models extremely polygonal and glitched. The ending was a tiny bit confusing but a nice throw back to the original game. would recommend for any hello neighbor fans looking for a good game to play.

Bought for my son but I can’t stop playing it and he watches on his iPad as I play. Cannot do the screws on the engine in the garage though !! Spent half hour trying to get them to stay green. Helppppppp is there a nack!??

This game doesn’t run well at all.

The graphics are low res and lack detail, yet the game continually stutters and hitches as objects and textures pop in and out.  As another reviewer mentioned, this sluggish performance can cause motion sickness. I’m pretty much immune to motion sickness in VR and even I started to feel unwell after a few minutes. I ran it on the Quest 3. I imagine it would perform even worse on the Quest 2.

VR interactions are primitive and sloppy. Objects like baseball bats and crow bars that you should be able to hold with both hands can only be held with one. Simple tasks like using a crowbar are frustrating. Throwing a basketball seems impossible unless it requires a button press or something. Trying to throw it the way you naturally would throw a basketball results in it just falling out of your hand.

Finally after playing for probably less than 15 minutes I hit a glitch that would require a restart.  Instead of restarting the game, I uninstalled it which was a relief at that point.

Being unfamiliar with the source material there’s absolutely nothing to compel me to ever play this again. Maybe Hello Neighbor fans could get some enjoyment out of it by suffering through the primitive gameplay, glitches, and motion sickness.

I’m requesting a refund.

reviews.review_title: Ignore fake reviews! Bugs are not fixed!

I am making a point to note this developer and never purchasing them forever. Ignore any reviews saying the bugs are fixed. They aren’t!

Do not under any circumstances buy this game. It’s impossible to play, I almost broke my back when I was just trying to climb up the children’s slide. I bought it for my child, she was upset because she couldn’t play it.

Update: no longer has any glitches so far, and it’s super fun! Ignore everything else in the old comment, it is all fixed. I love the way the developers responded to my old comment. Thank you! 

Old:Great idea, but you can’t really progress because of all of the glitches. Want to walk more than ten feet without flying into space? Not this game. I would recommend to wait for it to be fixed before you buy it.

Every time I get to the basement and go down the slide thing I get stuck in the floor and and when I manage to get past the area and when I open the door where I need a purple key and a blue key it teleport to me next to a TV

The game is is hands down FUN I can literally stay on it all day but don’t make a mistake and let your headset die or something else happen because this game does not save your progress. I think that’s horrible for the amount of money they want for this game. I paid 30.00 plus tax! I gave one star in order to write this review, otherwise it would not have gotten that! Too many glitches so I am writing this review today because I just played  this game and have been playing it for about a week now and today I have gotten really far in the game on this Sunday afternoon and I have lost all my progress! Not happy! So I would either like a refund for my money despite the rules for the return policy or the developer need to fix the glitches in the game. Thank you

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your review and the details about the issue experienced! We're happy to inform you that our most recent update has resolved many issues connected with stability & reliability. Now the number of scenarios when you lose progress should be much less. You can also try to save your progress manually as a backup for autosaves in the Tree House if you'd like. 

We recommend updating the game and jumping right back in the Tree House with the Rescue Squad! Please let us know if the problem has been resolved. Thank you for your support!

reviews.review_title: Good game, but a lot of bugs
reviews.review_description:
1. In the tutorial i had to restart the game because trinity wasnt appearing from the other side of the gate

2. After the tutorial i crouched down and tried to pick up a loose floor board thinking, “maybe i can jump down” then when i did i literally got flung across the map, and my screen went black

3. I unlocked the code for enzo’s part, and my arm got stuck in a wall + i put in the correct code and the door didnt open

Very disappointed, considering the fact i was hyped up for this game for months. I mean, the game is still playable, but im still confused on why you would release the game with all these bugs. Otherwise, good game.

Bought it for me to play on my Youtube channel, and got so hooked, I didn't even record! 

Pros And Cons:
Pros: 
1: Ai
2: Climbing
3: Toy Bonnie plush at the beginning
4: The Watch. It shows the irl time
5: The Beginning
6: Puzzles
7: Teleportation System, for people who do get Motion Sick. Thankfully, i do not get Motion Sick.

Cons:
1: Fall Damage. (I don't know if it's in the game, but still)
2:The Baseball Bat
3: At the verry beginning, you can grab a Bat and then turn on a baseball ball machine, where it shoots out Baseball Balls. But when you swing the Bat, the ball stays in the air.
Last one, 4: Hiding... sometimes when i switch character, the character just glitches out...

So that's it. I'm addicted already, and this game does need some patches here and there.

In Conclusion, lag is horrible, game gets boring, dumb things that make me lag. 6 out of 10, would not recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We are happy to inform you that our recent update has resolved several reported issues and improved stability and reliability. We suggest that you update your game and step into Mr. Peterson's house one more time! We are confident that you will appreciate the upgraded gaming experience. Please, let us know if your issue is fixed, and if you still encounter some problems.

reviews.review_description: The overall idea is great and much work was done to port this game to mobile platform. Thankfully the latest update fixed what was mostly broken after release. Hope for long time support for this outstanding game and even more optimization and content updates.

It sucks it keeps moving me to a different area when I am moving game graphics are good I guess but it’s just bad  I really hate that after you beat that long tutorial you just spawn in the neighbors house like come on just start at the base if you guys would have fixed that I would definitely be rateing this a 5 star

developer_response.body:
Thank you for providing feedback. We're pleased to announce that our latest update addresses many reported issues, including the ejection out of the world. We've implemented Yeet Manager - a new system that gracefully brings you back if you ever get ejected from the world. The game now treats physics-related much better in general.

If the issue persists even after installing the latest patch, kindly reach out to us at https://bit.ly/tBsupport. Our support team will require as much information from you as possible to assist you better.

See you in the Neighborhood!

reviews.review_title: Disappointed. I really wanted it to work
reviews.review_description:
I had to restart the game three times just to be able to get down to the backyard in the first scene. The Rope didn't work, at one point I lost my left hand and it didn't come back until I paused the game. Another instance, I was suspended in midair and then wouldn't drop to the ground until I paused and unpaused it and then died from the fall.... I have been a fan of this franchise for a while, and I was really hoping that the mistakes that were made with the DLC content on Hello Neighbors 2 weren't going to be repeated, but it has. I'm playing on a quest 2 and I'm very confused because when I go to buy the game on the quest store I'm told that the game isn't supported on quest 2 even though on the store page it says that it does.

I'll likely be returning the game if the bugs don't get fixed relatively soon. I just can't play the way it is right now because I'm taking damage and getting caught on account of the controls not working...

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We are happy to inform you that our recent update has resolved several reported issues and improved stability and reliability. Please, let us know if your issue is fixed, and if you still encounter some problems.

reviews.review_title: Do not buy this version, it's pretty bad
reviews.review_description:
I am tired of loading on to be flung around and floating. The quest 2 version is at least broken and this is not on the PC Oculus store and if it was, it would more than likely not be cross buy from what I am hearing.

  It appears to work on steam but I would avoid the Oculus Quest version, I have never seen a game in VR so broken and laggy in many parts of the game.

  I am reading that the development team does not fix their games, Oculus needs quality control on games. In VR, there is no room for games this glitchy that can cause health problems, they should not be making VR games.

  People can develop problems with VR games with issues like this one has and do not play this if you ever had seizures. The word is that the developers of this game also do not fix their games, usually most VR devs would have pushed an update sooner than a week after these problems. 

I would avoid this title as it stands and do not expect this game to get fixed.

developer_response.body:
Hi, and thank you for sharing your thoughts with us! We really appreciate you taking the time to do so. Rest assured that we always take your feedback into consideration.Thank you for providing feedback. We're pleased to announce that our latest update addresses many reported issues, including the ejection out of the world. We've implemented Yeet Manager - a new system that gracefully brings you back if you ever get ejected from the world. The game now treats physics-related much better in general.

Please update your game and give it another try. We continue working on stability and reliability, and we hope you’ll enjoy the improved experience! Be our Neighbor, and let us know if you are still experiencing the behavior you mentioned. See you in the Neighborhood!
